events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need identify component/condition: missing drip loop in service entrance & metering residential, rough-complete. Need decision fail because non-compliant/dangerous? Missing drip loop is a defect, can allow water into service head/metering, NEC 230.54 requires service heads/weatherproof service equipment with drip loops etc. So fail. Risk maybe high? Water ingress can cause arcing, equipment damage, safety. component_conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. percent_complete rough-complete maybe 80? value 0. findings: missing drip loop on service entrance conductors; no slack below service head to form drip loop; water can track into service head/raceway. actions: add drip loop with sufficient slack below service head per NEC 230.54, ensure conductors enter from below and loop below point of entry. references: NEC 230.54, 230.70? Also 230.26? Use Art. 230. Need include all fields. confidence 0.95. event_time 0, rate 0, order empty, workable empty, sound_source "". Let's craft JSON.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 85,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Service entrance conductors lack a drip loop below the service head.",
    "Missing slack loop allows water to track along conductors into the service head, weatherhead, or raceway.",
    "Condition violates weatherproofing requirements for service entrance assemblies."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Add a drip loop with adequate conductor slack below the service head so the loop is below the point of entry.",
    "Confirm conductors enter the service head from below and water will drip off the loop rather than run into the raceway or equipment.",
    "Re-inspect before energizing or closing up."
  ],
  "rationale": "NEC Article 230 requires service entrance conductors to be arranged so that water will not enter service raceways or equipment. A missing drip loop removes this protection, creating a high risk of water ingress, corrosion, and electrical failure.",
  "references": ["NEC Art. 230.54", "NEC Art. 230.70"]
}
